visualizations.title: Why Decimal Places Combine
visualizations.description: Fractions explain why decimal places add up in multiplication.

steps.explanation: Convert decimals to fractions: 0.12 has 2 places (hundredths), and 0.3 has 1 place (tenths).
Multiply numerators and denominators: 100 × 10 = 1000 (thousandths, which is 3 places).
Convert back to decimal. Notice the 3 places in the final product.

visualizations.title: Grid Multiplication of 0.8 × 0.6
visualizations.description: Shading 8 tenths horizontally and 6 tenths vertically shows that the product is the overlapping area of 48/100 (0.48).
